title: The Rise of Remote Work
passage: Remote work, also known as working from home, has become increasingly popular. Many companies now allow their employees to work from a location outside the traditional office. This offers several benefits. For employees, it can mean more flexibility in their schedules and less time spent commuting. This can lead to a better work-life balance. For employers, remote work can sometimes reduce office costs, such as rent and utilities.  However, remote work also presents challenges. It can be difficult for some people to stay focused and motivated when they are not in a shared workspace. Communication can also be a hurdle, as spontaneous conversations in an office are harder to replicate online.  Despite these challenges, many studies suggest that remote work can be just as productive, if not more productive, than working in an office, especially when the right tools and strategies are in place.

vocabulary.definition: more and more over a period of time
existing in or as part of a tradition; normal or conventional
the ability to change or be changed easily
the journey to and from work
make smaller or less in amount, degree, or size
having a strong reason to do something
achieving a lot; producing a lot of something

questions.question: Remote work means working from a different country.
Working from home can save employees time on travel.
Remote work always leads to lower productivity.
Which of the following is a benefit of remote work for employees?

ieltsTips: Read the passage carefully before answering the questions.
Pay attention to keywords in the questions and find them in the passage.
For True/False/Not Given questions, look for explicit information in the text. If the information is not there, choose 'Not Given'.
For multiple-choice questions, read all options before selecting the best answer.
For short answer questions, make sure your answer is concise and directly answers the question.
